sanskrit: भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च। अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा॥
transliteration: bhūmir āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṃ mano buddhir eva ca | ahaṅkāra itīyaṃ me bhinnā prakṛtir aṣṭadhā ||
literal_meaning: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intelligence and false-ego—these eight are My separated material energies.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the eight building-blocks of ordinary experience: five gross elements plus mind, intellect, and the ‘I-maker’ (ahaṅkāra).  They are ‘separated’ (bhinnā) from Him yet still His energies, inviting Arjuna to see every perception, thought, and self-image as contingent, composite, and not ultimately ‘Self’.
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion: the client begins to observe body, thoughts, and self-concept as ‘objects’ rather than the subject, loosening fusion with depressive or anxious narratives.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional in burnout realizes, ‘I am not my résumé, my body, or my anxiety; these are just passing phenomena,’ and feels both terror and freedom.
anchor_text: See body, thoughts, and ego as passing energies, not your true Self.
